title: Weekly Planning Session Frequency and Goal Achievement Rate Survey
description: This questionnaire measures how regularly a person plans their week and how effectively they achieve planned goals.

questions.text: How often do you dedicate time to holding a structured weekly planning session?
When you set goals at the start of a week, how many do you typically achieve by the end of it?
How clearly do you define your goals before beginning a planning session?

options.label: Always clearly, with specific steps and deadlines
I do not define goals in advance

questions.text: How consistently do you review what you planned at the end of each week to assess your progress?
When you do not achieve a planned goal, how do you typically respond?

options.label: I analyse what went wrong and adjust my approach
I reschedule the goal for next week
I feel frustrated but do not take specific action
I tend to abandon the goal altogether

questions.text: How well do you prioritise your goals during a planning session?

options.label: Very well, I always rank tasks by importance and urgency
Fairly well, I consider priority most of the time
Inconsistently, I sometimes treat all tasks as equally important
I do not prioritise and proceed without a clear order

questions.text: How often do you break large goals into smaller, manageable steps during planning?
How realistic are the goals you usually set for a single week?
How often do unexpected events completely derail your weekly plans?

options.label: Almost never because I adapt well

questions.text: How much time do you usually spend preparing your weekly plan?

options.label: Enough time to think through tasks carefully
I do not set aside planning time

questions.text: How often do you write your weekly goals down in a planner, notebook, or digital tool?
How confident do you feel that you will complete your goals after a planning session?
When planning your week, how often do you consider deadlines in advance?
How often do you include time for rest or recovery when making your weekly plan?
How likely are you to postpone an important task even after planning it?
How often do you adjust your weekly goals when you notice they are no longer realistic?

options.label: I usually continue without adjusting anything

questions.text: How organized are the tools or systems you use for weekly planning?

options.label: Very organized and easy to follow
I do not use any consistent system

questions.text: How often do you begin your week knowing exactly which goals are the top priority?
How often do you reflect on why certain goals were completed successfully?
Overall, how effective do you believe your weekly planning process is?

states.label: Highly Effective Planner
states.summary: Exceptional planning frequency and goal achievement.
states.description: Strong self-regulatory habits are visible in the answers, so no immediate intervention is needed.

states.label: Effective Planner
states.summary: Good planning consistency with solid goal achievement.
states.description: Current habits are working well, and only minor improvements are likely to be needed.

states.label: Moderate Planner
states.summary: Planning is present, but goal achievement is inconsistent.
states.description: Refining goal structure, planning detail, and weekly review habits would likely improve results.

states.label: Inconsistent Planner
states.summary: Planning sessions are irregular and goal achievement is low.
states.description: Increasing planning frequency and adding stronger accountability would be advisable.

states.label: Disengaged Planner
states.summary: There is little structured planning and planned goals are rarely achieved.
states.description: A more supportive structure around self-regulation and goal setting is strongly recommended.
